question_text:
3Sum With Multiplicity

Given an integer array arr, and an integer target, return the number of tuples i, j, k such that i < j < k and arr[i] + arr[j] + arr[k] == target.

Since the answer can be very large, return it modulo 109 + 7.

Constraints:

  • 3 <= arr.length <= 3000
  • 0 <= arr[i] <= 100
  • 0 <= target <= 300
input_format: An integer array arr and an integer target.
output_format: The number of index triplets (i,j,k) with i<j<k such that arr[i]+arr[j]+arr[k]=target, modulo 10^9+7.
explanation:
Counting with Frequency Map

Use a frequency count array c of size 101. Iterate over all pairs (j, k) where j < k. For each pair, check if (target - arr[j] - arr[k]) exists in elements before j using a running count. Alternatively, fix the two larger values (j, k) and count c[target-arr[j]-arr[k]] accumulated from left to right in O(N²) total.

Time: O(N²). Space: O(1).
